title: leontodon taraxacum var. vulgare
intro: Leontodon taraxacum var. vulgare, commonly known as dandelion, is a perennial plant with a long history of use in herbal medicine, identified by its bright yellow flowers and jagged leaves. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are rich in medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Leontodon taraxacum var. vulgare include its ability to support liver and kidney function, aid in digestion, and lower blood pressure, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used fresh in salads or cooked as a vegetable, allowing for a range of applications in herbal medicine.
